---
title: Tutorials
sidebar: tutorials
---

The tutorials below are executable Jupyter notebooks demonstrating PaleoBeasts
models and utilities. Each notebook can be run locally after
[installing the package](../get-started/installation.qmd).

## Getting started

| Notebook | What it shows |
|---|---|
| [PBModel basics](../../notebooks/base_classes/pbmodel_basics.ipynb) | Base class interface: construction, `param_values`, integration, `PBOutput`, callable parameters, `register_forcing`, `set_function`, copying, `to_pyleo` |
| [Forcing](../../notebooks/base_classes/forcing.ipynb) | All four `Forcing` construction patterns; `Hold`, `Ramp`, `Harmonic` elements; bundled datasets; `register_forcing` options |

## Model demos

These notebooks each cover a single model: setup, parameters, forcing, and output.

| Notebook | Model | What it shows |
|---|---|---|
| [Stommel THC](../../notebooks/model_demos/stommel.ipynb) | `Stommel` | Two-box thermohaline circulation, overturning diagnostic, two forcing attachment styles |
| [EBM0D](../../notebooks/model_demos/ebm0d.ipynb) | `EBM0D` | Zero-dimensional EBM, albedo options (constant / `albedo_func` / custom callable), OLR, time-varying parameters, Euler vs RK45 |
| [EBM1DLat](../../notebooks/model_demos/ebm1d_lat.ipynb) | `EBM1DLat` | Diffusive latitudinal EBM, ice-line, multi-axis latitude profiles, custom albedo via `set_function` |

## Functionality demos

| Notebook | What it shows |
|---|---|
| [Noise](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/noise_demo.ipynb) | Generating colored noise and AR(1) surrogates |
| [Model noise](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/model_noise_demo.ipynb) | Adding stochastic noise to model output |
| [Downsampling](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/downsample_demo.ipynb) | Simulating irregular sampling from a model trajectory |
